puretorch.nn package#

Submodules#

puretorch.nn.functional module#

puretorch.nn.functional.cross_entropy(logits: Tensor, targets: ndarray | Tensor, ignore_idx: int = -100, weight: ndarray | List | Tensor | None = None, reduction: Literal['mean', 'sum'] | None = 'mean') → Tensor[source]#
Cross-entropy over logits […, C] and targets:

  • class indices […] or

  • one-hot / soft probs […, C]

Parameters:

  • logits (Tensor) – tensor of size [B,C]

  • targets (Tensor) – tensor of size [B,]

  • ignore_idx (int) – target value that is ignored during loss calculation

  • weight (Tensor, optional) – a manual rescaling weight given to each class. If given, it has to be a Tensor of size C. Otherwise, it is treated as if having all ones.

  • reduction (str, optional) – reduction applied to output

Returns:

Tensor with loss as float

puretorch.nn.functional.log_softmax(logits: Tensor, dim: int = -1) → Tensor[source]#
puretorch.nn.functional.relu(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]#
puretorch.nn.functional.softmax(logits: Tensor, dim: int = -1) → Tensor[source]#
puretorch.nn.functional.tanh(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]#

puretorch.nn.linear module#

class puretorch.nn.linear.Linear(in_features: int, out_features: int, bias: bool = True)[source]#

Bases: Module

forward(x)[source]#

Performs a forward pass using the layers weights and bias

Parameters:

layer (x - input to)

Returns:

list of outputs of the layer

puretorch.nn.module module#

class puretorch.nn.module.Module[source]#

Bases: object

Base module class. Handles module’s train/eval modes only (as of now).

add_module(name: str, module: Parameter)[source]#
children() → Iterator[Module][source]#
eval(mode: bool = True)[source]#
forward(*args, **kwargs)[source]#
parameters() → Iterator[Parameter][source]#
register_param(name: str, param: Parameter)[source]#
train(mode: bool = True)[source]#

Current behavior: toggle requires_grad for all parameters (recursively) and set ._training flag. (You can later specialize for dropout/BN.)

puretorch.nn.parameter module#

class puretorch.nn.parameter.Parameter(data, requires_grad: bool = True)[source]#

Bases: Tensor

Simple alias to mark a Tensor as trainable parameter.

puretorch.nn.relu module#

class puretorch.nn.relu.ReLU[source]#

Bases: Module

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]#

puretorch.nn.sequential module#

class puretorch.nn.sequential.Sequential(*modules: Module, name: str = 'Sequential model')[source]#

Bases: Module

forward(x: Tensor) → Tensor[source]#

Performs a forward pass on the input Tensor and returns a Tensor with the output of the model :param x: Tensor to propagate on.

Returns:

A Tensor with the output of the model on the input Tensor (x).

Base module class. Handles module’s train/eval modes only (as of now).

Current behavior: toggle requires_grad for all parameters (recursively) and set ._training flag. (You can later specialize for dropout/BN.)

class puretorch.nn.Parameter(data, requires_grad: bool = True)[source]#

Bases: Tensor

Simple alias to mark a Tensor as trainable parameter.
